mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single month, it triggers a flight-to-safety rotation out of equities into fixed income, depressing S&P 500 prices over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the cross-sectional correlation between UNRATE changes and SPX returns exceeds 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of real personal savings (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals a synchronized withdrawal of household balance sheet support, which reduces aggregate consumption demand and forces equity multiples to contract over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS decline × HOUST decline) fails to explain more than 5% of the variance in 20-day SPX returns, or the joint signal predicts negative returns but SPX rallies in > 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets, an acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects capacity expansion that outpaces demand, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle capacity expansion with tight labor
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns are not significantly lower when INDPRO accelerates above its 12-month moving average during high PAYEMS growth periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) exceeding 3.0% annualized rate sustains corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as institutional investors price in continued macroeconomic strength.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 surprises, or the rolling Spearman rank correlation falls below 0.05 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.0%, the market prices in a higher probability of prolonged restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not decline when the threshold is breached, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows rapidly while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) fails to decline indicates a labor market that is absorbing workers without reducing slack, signaling structural inflationary pressure that forces the Fed to maintain higher rates and drags on equity valuations over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 40% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a sudden drop in housing starts (HOUST) acts as a leading indicator for broader consumer demand contraction, causing a rapid repricing of cyclical equities and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not significantly negative when HOUST drops sharply during high CPI periods, or the signal produces false positives in > 50% of test cases.
